article_title: CISA Adds the First-Ever Splunk Flaw to Its KEV Catalog, an Unauthenticated File-Write Bug in a PostgreSQL Sidecar Now Exploited in the Wild
summary: Every Rule 9 specific (CVSS 9.8, vector, CWE-306, version ranges, CVE id, June 18 KEV add, June 21 deadline, first-ever Splunk KEV) verified verbatim against NVD and the official Splunk advisory; the only finding is an allowlist-coverage gap on two reputable, fully corroborated sources.

content_preview.title: CISA Adds the First-Ever Splunk Flaw to Its KEV Catalog, an Unauthenticated File-Write Bug in a PostgreSQL Sidecar Now Exploited in the Wild
content_preview.summary: CVE-2026-20253, a CVSS 9.8 missing-authentication flaw in Splunk Enterprise's PostgreSQL sidecar, became the first Splunk bug on CISA's KEV list, with federal agencies ordered to patch by June 21.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency has added a critical Splunk Enterprise vulnerability to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, the first time a Splunk flaw has appeared on the list, according to [SOCRadar](https://socradar.io/blog/cve-2026-20253-cisa-splunk-enterprise-rce/). The bug, tracked as CVE-2026-20253, lets an unauthenticated attacker create or truncate arbitrary files through a PostgreSQL sidecar service endpoint, and Splunk has acknowl...

recommendations: Consider adding advisory.splunk.com and socradar.io to config/source_allowlist.txt; the official Splunk PSIRT advisory and SOCRadar are both reputable security sources used here for facts already corroborated by NVD and SecurityWeek.

editor_notes.content_quality: Tightly scoped 535-word News piece with a clean Overview / What We Know / What We Don't Know / Analysis structure. Technical depth (CVSS vector, CWE-306, version ranges, PoC-to-RCE chaining) is accurate and appropriately explained. The 'What We Don't Know' section honestly bounds the reporting (no attribution of the in-the-wild activity, no compromise counts, no exposure figures).
editor_notes.source_verification: Read all 5 gzipped snapshots from disk and verified every cited claim. source-0 (NVD, nvd.nist.gov): confirms CVE-2026-20253, CVSS base 9.8, CWE-306 'Missing Authentication for Critical Function', vector 'CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H', the verbatim root-cause quote ('the PostgreSQL sidecar service endpoint lacks authentication controls, allowing any network-reachable user to invoke file operations without credentials'), and 'Splunk Enterprise versions 9.4 and earlier are not affected'. source-1 (official Splunk advisory SVD-2026-0603, advisory.splunk.com): confirms Published 2026-06-10, Score 9.8 Critical, identical vector, CWE-306, affected '10.2.0 to 10.2.3' and '10.0.0 to 10.0.6', fix versions 10.4.0/10.2.4/10.0.7, and PSIRT awareness of limited exploitation in June 2026. source-2 (SecurityWeek): confirms 'CISA added CVE-2026-20253 to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog on June 18 and instructed federal agencies to address it by June 21', 'This is the first Splunk flaw added to CISA's KEV list', patches June 10, and 'Two days after its disclosure...WatchTowr demonstrated how CVE-2026-20253 can be exploited by an unauthenticated attacker for remote code execution'. source-3 (SOCRadar): confirms 'first Splunk vulnerability ever added to the KEV list', 'CVSS 9.8...missing authentication flaw (CWE-306)', and 'watchTowr Labs published a technical write-up and proof-of-concept (PoC) exploit code on June 12'. source-4 (Help Net Security): confirms the Splunk-attributed quote 'an unauthenticated user could create or truncate arbitrary files through a PostgreSQL sidecar service endpoint' and 'CVE-2026-20253 can be used by attackers to execute arbitrary code and achieve full control over the Splunk application environment'. Every snapshot supported the claim attributed to it; no misattribution and no hallucinated quotes.
editor_notes.rule9_compliance: Rule 9 satisfied. The CVSS 9.8 score and CVSS:3.1 vector, the CWE-306 classification, and the exact affected/fixed version ranges all trace to NVD (source-0) and the official Splunk advisory SVD-2026-0603 (source-1). The CVE id (CVE-2026-20253), the June 18 KEV add date, the June 21 federal patch deadline, and the 'first-ever Splunk KEV' framing each appear verbatim in cited sources (NVD, Splunk advisory, SecurityWeek, SOCRadar).
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All claims align with cited sources. The article correctly attributes the unverified-attribution caveats. Version ranges rendered as 'through' rather than the advisory's 'to' are an accurate synonym, not a discrepancy. The 'roughly a week later' / 'same-week federal deadline' framing in Analysis is consistent with the June 10 patch / June 18 KEV / June 21 deadline timeline.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. The Analysis section is interpretive but grounded ('partly symbolic and partly operational', compressed-timeline observation) and avoids sensationalism. No AI self-reference.
editor_notes.originality: New topic. Recent KEV coverage exists (SolarWinds Serv-U, Oracle PeopleSoft, Linux cgroups, BOD 26-04) but none cover CVE-2026-20253 or the Splunk flaw; this is the first-ever Splunk KEV entry and a distinct story.
concerns: Two cited sources — advisory.splunk.com (the official Splunk PSIRT advisory) and socradar.io — are not yet in config/source_allowlist.txt, which produced the automated warning. Both are reputable, and every fact they support is independently corroborated by the allowlisted NVD and SecurityWeek sources. This is a config-coverage gap, not a credibility or accuracy issue, and does not warrant a public corrections record.
recommendations: Add advisory.splunk.com and socradar.io to the source allowlist to suppress the spurious warning on future security reporting.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: High-quality, accurate, well-sourced security News piece. Every Rule 9 specific verified verbatim against primary sources (NVD and the official Splunk advisory). The sole automated finding is an allowlist-coverage gap on two reputable, fully corroborated sources, which is not a factual defect and does not require a corrections trail. Verdict revised from the script's auto-default APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS to APPROVE. Ready for publication.
